nodes.name: InitializeNUTSState
nodes.description: Build immutable NUTS state from the target log-density, initial position, acceptance target, and explicit RNG key state.

inputs.name: seed
inputs.constraints: Used to derive deterministic PRNGKey/RNG state

outputs.name: nuts_state
outputs.constraints: Immutable state object; no hidden mutation

outputs.name: rng_key
outputs.constraints: Explicit stochastic state threaded across calls

nodes.type_signature: (target: Callable oracle: position -> log_prob, initial_positions: Array[float], target_accept_p: float, seed: int) -> tuple[StateModelSpec{position, momentum, mass_matrix, trace}, PRNGKey]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Deterministic Stochastic-State Constructor. Constructs an immutable initialization record for a gradient-informed stochastic transition process from a pure scalar-field oracle, an initial coordinate vector, a control scalar, and an integer seed. The seed is transformed into an explicit pseudorandom generator state so all randomness is externally threaded and reproducible. The output bundles primary coordinates with auxiliary kinetic/metric and trace containers needed for subsequent state transitions without hidden mutation.. Applications: Initializing reproducible latent-state exploration in high-dimensional inverse problems for medical imaging reconstruction, Bootstrapping stochastic trajectory search for molecular conformation landscapes in computational chemistry, Setting up probabilistic parameter-space traversal for aerodynamic model calibration in aerospace engineering, Creating explicit random-state simulation seeds and immutable transition records for uncertainty quantification in power-grid dynamics

nodes.name: RunNUTSTransitions
nodes.description: Apply NUTS transition kernels for warmup/discard and collection while returning new immutable chain state, diagnostics trace, and split RNG key.

inputs.name: rng_key_in
inputs.constraints: Must be consumed/split and returned as new key

inputs.name: log_prob_oracle
inputs.constraints: finite log-density evaluator used by tree transitions

nodes.type_signature: (nuts_state_in: StateModelSpec{position, logp, gradient, target_accept_p, step_size}, rng_key_in: PRNGKey, n_collect: int, n_discard: int, log_prob_oracle: Callable[[NDArray[np.float64]], float], max_tree_depth: int = 6) -> tuple[Array[float], Array[Diagnostics], StateModelSpec, PRNGKey]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Adaptive Gradient-Informed Markov Transition Executor. Iteratively applies a stochastic, gradient-informed transition kernel to an immutable state in two phases: adaptation-only steps (discarded) followed by retained steps (collected). Each iteration advances latent continuous variables and auxiliary dynamics variables while appending per-step diagnostics, producing a new state object rather than mutating the input. Randomness is handled through explicit pseudo-random key splitting so execution remains reproducible and functionally pure.. Applications: Calibrating latent parameter fields in inverse problems for geophysical tomography, Sampling high-dimensional conformational states in molecular or materials simulation, Estimating latent dynamical states and hyperparameters in nonlinear control-system identification, Uncertainty-aware reconstruction of volumetric images in computational medical imaging

nodes.name: AllocatePerChainStatChannels
nodes.description: Create one sender/receiver channel pair per chain so each worker can stream progress snapshots independently.

nodes.name: SpawnProgressMonitor
nodes.description: Start a monitor task that consumes chain stats, updates per-chain/global progress state, computes acceptance and rhat summaries, and exits when all chains finish.

inputs.name: stats_receivers
inputs.constraints: all channels correspond to active chains

nodes.type_signature: (stats_receivers: Vec<Receiver<ChainStats>>, n_collect: usize, n_discard: usize) -> JoinHandle<()>

nodes.name: RunChainsInScopedParallel
nodes.description: Execute each chain in a scoped thread, forwarding progress through its sender, and collect one 2D sample tensor per chain.
nodes.type_signature: (chains: Vec<Chain<B>>, stats_senders: Vec<Sender<ChainStats>>, n_collect: usize, n_discard: usize) -> Vec<Tensor<B, 2>>

nodes.name: StackChainSamples
nodes.description: Aggregate per-chain 2D tensors into one 3D tensor along the chain axis.

nodes.name: JoinProgressMonitor
nodes.description: Wait for the monitor task to finish and produce a join status used for optional error reporting.

nodes.name: ComputeRunStatsFromSamples
nodes.description: Materialize sample data, form a 3D ndarray view, and derive run-level diagnostics/statistics.

inputs.name: sample_tensor
inputs.constraints: data convertible to contiguous f32 slice

nodes.name: AssembleRunProgressResult
nodes.description: Construct the final success result containing stacked samples and derived run statistics.

inputs.name: monitor_join_status
inputs.constraints: used only for optional logging; does not alter success path

nodes.type_signature: (sample_tensor: Tensor<B, 3>, run_stats: RunStats, monitor_join_status: Result<(), Box<dyn Any + Send>>) -> Result<(Tensor<B, 3>, RunStats), Box<dyn Error>>
